event: Bretton Woods: 44 nations peg their currencies to the US dollar and the dollar to gold at a fixed $35/oz, while the US holds ~20,000 tons of gold, about two-thirds of world reserves
note: Fixed gold price anchored the postwar monetary system but capped gold and gave the US sole money-printing privilege, from YouTube analysis videos

event: France under de Gaulle calls Bretton Woods a fraud and redeems $150M of dollars for US gold, followed by West Germany, Switzerland and the Netherlands
note: Foreign central banks converting dollar reserves drained US gold vaults and exposed the gap between dollars outstanding and gold backing, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Aug 15, 1971: Nixon 'temporarily' suspends dollar-gold convertibility, ending the $35/oz peg, after ~$80B of dollars abroad stood against only ~$10B of gold in US vaults
note: With reserves projected to run out within about 18 months, closing the gold window turned the dollar into pure fiat and freed gold to float, from YouTube analysis videos

event: US-Saudi petrodollar agreement: Saudi Arabia sells oil exclusively in dollars and recycles proceeds into US securities in exchange for military protection, later joined by other OPEC states
note: Structural oil demand for dollars replaced gold convertibility as the anchor of dollar dominance, capping the flight from paper money, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Gold climbs from the $35/oz 1971 peg to over $600/oz by 1980, a ~17x rise, as post-gold-standard inflation erodes the dollar
note: Unanchored money printing and 1970s double-digit inflation drove savers from depreciating paper dollars into gold, from YouTube analysis videos

event: The 1985 Plaza Accord deliberately weakens the dollar and the yen surges far faster than expected; to avert an export recession the Bank of Japan cuts its rate to a record-low 2.5% by February 1987
note: Ultra-cheap credit plus relaxed 'window guidance' lending quotas flooded land and stock markets with borrowed money, igniting Japan's asset bubble; from YouTube analysis videos

event: Black Monday, Oct 19, 1987: Dow falls 508 points (-22.6%), the largest one-day percentage drop ever, nearly double 1929's Black Tuesday (-12.8%)
Oct 14-16, 1987: an anti-takeover tax bill and a worse-than-expected trade deficit send the S&P down over 9% in the week before Black Monday
Portfolio insurance models auto-sold as prices fell: about $6B of $31B in Black Monday sell orders (~19%), creating a self-reinforcing selling feedback loop
On Black Monday 11 of 30 Dow stocks opened over an hour late; stale quotes made futures look cheap, and index-arbitrage sell orders piled onto the NYSE
CME margin calls hit ~10x normal size on Black Monday; margin was collected before offsetting gains were credited, forcing traders into loans and liquidations
Oct 20, 1987: Fed publicly pledges liquidity and companies announce buybacks; Dow posts record daily gains and recovers fully in under two years; circuit breakers are introduced
The 1987 crash goes global: Hong Kong suspends trading for a week and its futures market needs a government rescue; New Zealand loses ~60%, bottoming in Feb 1988

At the bubble's height the grounds of Tokyo's Imperial Palace are valued above all real estate in California, golf club memberships total about $200B, and over half the profits of Toyota and Nissan come from financial speculation ('zaitech'), not operations
note: Companies revalued land holdings as hidden assets and banks lent against inflated collateral, a feedback loop detaching prices from fundamentals; 1985-1989 land and stocks rose about 240-245%; from YouTube analysis videos

event: Nikkei peaks at 38,915 on December 29, 1989 after stocks rose roughly three times faster than corporate earnings; Nomura Securities predicts 80,000 by 1995
note: Peak euphoria: forecasts extrapolated the bubble even as valuations had detached from earnings, marking the exact top for 34 years; from YouTube analysis videos

event: New BOJ governor Mieno, aiming to 'prick the bubble', orders a rate hike on Christmas Day 1989 - the first of five raising rates from 2.5% to 6% by August 1990; the Nikkei drops 32% in 1990, erasing over $2 trillion
note: Deliberate monetary tightening plus 1989 caps on real-estate lending cut off the cheap credit sustaining asset prices, deflating the bubble gradually rather than overnight; from YouTube analysis videos

event: By 1992 the Nikkei is down about 60% from its 1989 peak and Tokyo property prices fall roughly 60% within a few years, leaving banks with collateral worth a fraction of their loans
note: Collapsed collateral turned bank books into piles of non-performing loans, starting Japan's deflationary 'lost decades' of near-zero growth; from YouTube analysis videos

event: Netscape IPO doubles on day one, hitting a $2.7B market cap in minutes despite losing money, igniting the dot-com IPO wave
Hokkaido Takushoku becomes the first major Japanese bank to fail in 1997, as 'zombie loans' - loans to insolvent borrowers kept alive with rate breaks and forgiveness - overwhelm bank balance sheets
note: Banks propping up unprofitable 'zombie' companies delayed the reckoning for years; cleaning zombie loans off balance sheets took another six to ten years and government rescues; from YouTube analysis videos

event: China ends free socialist housing allocation; banks start issuing mortgages and urban population climbs from ~415M toward 900M+, launching the world's biggest property boom
With rates near 0% by 1998 and prices still falling, Japan hits a liquidity trap; the BOJ pioneers forward guidance and becomes the first central bank to use quantitative easing
note: Conventional policy was exhausted, so the BOJ bought assets from banks for cash to force money into the economy - the playbook the US later copied in 2008; results in Japan were modest; from YouTube analysis videos

event: Y2K fears drive a computer-upgrade spending wave; 1999 tech IPOs post 400-700% first-day pops and some stocks gain over 1,000% for the year
Pets.com falls from a $300M+ market cap to zero within 268 days; roughly half of all internet companies eventually fail
Gold languishes near 20-year lows around $280/oz
note: central bank sales, strong dollar, equity mania

event: Dot-com bubble peaks: NASDAQ hits 5,048 on Mar 10, then collapses
note: NASDAQ -39% in 2000, start of 3-year bear market

event: Mar 13, 2000: news that Japan entered recession, on top of planned Fed tightening, deals Nasdaq one of its largest losses and starts the dot-com bust
Greece adopts the euro in 2001 after Goldman Sachs currency swaps mask its debt load to meet Maastricht criteria; borrowing costs fall from ~18% toward German levels
note: Shared currency made lenders treat Greek debt as backed by the whole eurozone, unleashing cheap credit and deficit spending; from YouTube analysis videos

event: 9/11 attacks: NYSE closed 4 days, Dow -7.1% on reopening
note: worst weekly Dow loss since 1933 at the time

event: Fed cuts rates 11 times as US enters recession
note: S&P 500 -13% in 2001, second straight losing year

event: Enron/WorldCom accounting scandals deepen bear market
note: investor trust in corporate earnings collapses

event: NASDAQ bottoms at 1,114, down 78% from 2000 peak
Gold ends year above $340 as weak dollar starts new bull market
note: first strong yearly gain since mid-1990s

event: Iraq War begins; markets bottom and rally strongly
Gold breaks $400/oz for first time since 1996
Yield-hungry global investors pour into US mortgage-backed securities; lenders loosen standards and issue subprime, adjustable-rate and NINJA loans to feed demand, pushing home prices up
Dot-com aftermath: about 415,000 IT jobs lost, half of dot-coms dead by 2004, and the Nasdaq needs 15 years to regain its 2000 peak
SEC loosens net capital rule: investment banks over $5B in assets freed from 12:1 leverage cap, enabling unlimited leverage in the shadow banking system
May 14, 2004: Nifty plunges ~7.9% (Sensex -6.1%) after the Congress-led UPA's surprise election win over the expected BJP result
note: An unpriced political outcome plus fears of a coalition dependent on left parties extended the selloff over following sessions, from YouTube analysis videos

event: US house-flipping mania peaks: homes treated as lottery tickets, zero-down loans common, prices doubling in months in hot areas as speculation feeds on itself
Nikkei surges ~40% on Koizumi reforms and economic recovery
Gold breaks $500/oz for first time since 1987
note: investment demand era begins (gold ETFs launched 2004)

event: Gold spikes to $730, a 26-year high, then corrects sharply
Over two dozen subprime lenders including New Century go bust; subprime is only 13% of mortgages but about half of all foreclosures
Two Bear Stearns hedge funds implode on bad mortgage debt and freeze withdrawals; banks grow afraid to lend to each other
Credit crunch begins: BNP Paribas freezes subprime funds
note: first visible crack of the financial crisis

event: S&P 500 peaks at 1,565 as subprime losses spread
note: top before the global financial crisis

event: Shanghai Composite bubble peaks at 6,124
By October 2007 3% of all US home loans are in foreclosure and another 7% a month past due; banks flood the market with foreclosed homes, tipping supply past demand
US home prices fall about 28% nationally; negative-equity owners walk away, adding supply and deepening the spiral — ultimately ~4M foreclosures and $19T of household wealth lost
Athens Stock Exchange plummets 65% in 2008 as the global recession hits Greece's tourism and shipping-dependent economy
note: Small open economy with high debt had no independent monetary policy inside the euro to cushion the shock; from YouTube analysis videos

event: Gold tops $1,000/oz for first time as Bear Stearns collapses
Lehman Brothers bankruptcy triggers global financial crisis
US government seizes Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac on Sep 6; the two guaranteed about half of all US home loans
AIG rescued with $85B Fed loan day after Lehman; it had sold credit default swaps on mortgage securities without capital to back them
Nikkei crashes to 26-year low in global panic selling
Congress passes $700B TARP bailout as credit markets freeze; Dow down nearly 40% from peak by October
Volkswagen 'mother of all squeezes': shares jump from ~197 in September to ~999 in October 2008 as trapped short sellers are forced to cover, costing hedge funds ~$30B
note: Porsche's stake left too little free float for shorts to buy back, the historical template later cited for GameStop, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Nifty falls ~52% in 2008 amid global crisis and FII outflows
note: worst year in Indian market history

event: S&P 500 bottoms at 666 intraday; historic bull market begins
note: Fed QE and stimulus fuel V-shaped equity recovery

event: May 18, 2009: Nifty surges ~17.7% on the UPA's re-election, hitting the upper circuit breaker and forcing trading to halt mid-session
note: A decisive mandate without left-party dependence removed political risk at once; circuit breakers capped the one-day repricing, from YouTube analysis videos

event: New Greek government reveals the 2009 budget deficit is 12.7% of GDP, roughly double the previously reported 6.7%; rating agencies begin cutting Greece toward junk
note: Discovery of falsified statistics destroyed sovereign credibility and sent borrowing costs skyrocketing; from YouTube analysis videos

event: Gold sets record highs above $1,200 on QE and dollar debasement fears
On April 27, 2010, with debt-to-GDP at 127%, Greece's credit rating is cut to junk, pushing the country toward default and forcing the first EUR 110B troika bailout days later
note: Junk status locked Greece out of bond markets, making external rescue the only alternative to default; from YouTube analysis videos

event: Flash Crash: Dow drops ~1,000 points in minutes
Greek bailout marks start of eurozone sovereign debt crisis
note: periphery yields spike; US stocks still gain in 2010

event: SEC investigation traces the May 6, 2010 flash crash to a single $4.1B sell order of 75,000 E-mini S&P 500 futures contracts executed by an algorithm with no price or time limits
note: A trade that would normally be worked over ~5 hours was dumped as fast as possible, overwhelming futures liquidity and dragging broad indices down several percent within minutes; from YouTube analysis videos

event: During the flash crash high-frequency traders pass contracts among themselves in a 'hot potato' while market-maker algorithms hit risk limits and withdraw; blue chips like Accenture print at $0.01 and other stocks at $100,000
note: When fast markets triggered pre-programmed exits, liquidity evaporated and only stub quotes remained, so thousands of absurd trades were later canceled; from YouTube analysis videos

event: During the May 6, 2010 flash crash the consolidated NYSE quote feed lags by up to 36 seconds, so the displayed Dow shows prices far from where the market actually trades
note: Firms paying for direct exchange feeds saw true prices and could arbitrage the same instrument quoted differently across venues - effectively risk-free profit from the malfunction; from YouTube analysis videos

event: CME's Stop Logic Functionality pauses E-mini trading for 5 seconds at 2:45pm on May 6, 2010, breaking the panic; the market recovers most of a ~$1 trillion loss within about 36 minutes
note: The brief halt let buyers re-enter at discount prices; the SEC then introduced single-stock circuit breakers pausing trading 5 minutes on a 10% move within 5 minutes; from YouTube analysis videos

event: Asian physical demand floors the market: bar premiums hit multi-year highs in China, India, Singapore; record January ETF outflow reverses to inflows by March
First bubble: BTC ran to ~$32 then collapsed toward $2; Mt.Gox hack sell order flash-crashed price from $17.50 to $0.01 in 30 minutes
Gold closes above $1,600 for first time as US debt-ceiling standoff stalls; University of Texas endowment takes delivery of $1B in physical bars
S&P downgrades US credit rating from AAA; markets plunge
Gold hits record $1,921/oz on euro crisis and US debt fears
Hackers copied Mt.Gox hot-wallet private keys in Sept 2011, starting a silent multi-year drain of ~630,000 BTC that caused the 2014 insolvency
Eurozone crisis escalates: DAX -14.7% in 2011, Italian yields above 7%
note: contagion fears for Italy and Spain

event: On February 21, 2012 the troika arranges the largest sovereign debt restructuring in history: private creditors take a 53.5% haircut alongside a second EUR 130B bailout
note: Writing down bonds cut the debt stock but austerity conditions deepened the recession, lifting debt-to-GDP to ~172%; from YouTube analysis videos

event: Draghi whatever-it-takes pledge halts euro crisis
First Bitcoin halving (block reward 50 to 25 BTC) in Nov 2012 with BTC near $11-12; within a year the price spiked to ~$1,100 as reduced new supply met rising demand.
US FinCEN guidance classified Bitcoin exchanges as money-transmitter businesses requiring costly state licenses, first US regulatory recognition of BTC as money
Cyprus banking crisis fuels first big rally; BTC hits ~$266 then crashes
Gold crashes 13% in two days; -28% for 2013, worst year in 32 years
note: ETF outflows, taper fears end the bull run

event: Winklevoss twins disclosed owning ~1% of all BTC; VC involvement (Draper, Andreessen) seen as legitimizing Bitcoin during first mainstream media coverage
During April crash from ~$266 Mt.Gox halted trading; media asked has the bubble popped, regulators could not classify Bitcoin
Taper tantrum: Bernanke hints at reducing QE, bonds and EM sell off
US DHS seized ~$5M from Mt.Gox accounts and froze its US banking channels, causing fiat withdrawal delays and eroding confidence
FBI shuts down Silk Road marketplace and seizes bitcoins
Bitcoin crosses $1,000 for the first time
People's Bank of China bars financial institutions from Bitcoin transactions
Abenomics: Nikkei +57%, best year since 1972, on BOJ mega-easing
S&P 500 +30% in 2013 on Fed liquidity and earnings recovery
Tether (USDT), the first stablecoin, was created by developers associated with the Bitfinex exchange in Hong Kong and went live on Bitfinex that year.
note: Built as exchange settlement infrastructure because Asian crypto exchanges lacked reliable dollar banking; became the liquidity backbone of crypto trading, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Mt.Gox exchange collapses; ~850,000 BTC reported lost
Feb 7: Mt.Gox (once ~70% of all BTC trading) froze BTC withdrawals blaming a transaction malleability bug, sparking price drop weeks before bankruptcy
Modi election victory: Nifty rallies to record highs, +31% in 2014
Ethereum crowdsale (ICO) raises ~$18M in BTC
Microsoft begins accepting Bitcoin payments
Oil price collapses ~50% in second half; energy stocks slump
note: consumers benefit, energy sector and Russia hit

event: Bitstamp hacked, ~19,000 BTC stolen; BTC bottoms near $170
Amazon discloses AWS financials for the first time; shares jump ~14%
note: validated cloud computing as a massive profit engine

event: Nearly five years after the event, the US Department of Justice charges a London-based futures trader with manipulating E-mini S&P 500 futures via spoofing, alleging he contributed to the 2010 flash crash
note: The case showed a single trader could have outsized influence on the $19T-cap S&P 500 through the futures market and revived calls to merge SEC and CFTC oversight; from YouTube analysis videos

event: Shanghai bubble bursts: index falls 40%+ from June peak by August
On June 30, 2015 Greece misses a EUR 1.6B IMF payment, the first developed country ever to default to the IMF; the government shuts the stock exchange and banking system
note: Failed anti-austerity negotiations triggered capital flight and a liquidity crisis, forcing capital controls until an EUR 86B third bailout in August; from YouTube analysis videos

event: Shanghai Composite gains over 150% in the year to its June 2015 peak of ~5,100 as some 90 million retail traders pile in, nearly half joining in the final year
note: State-media cheerleading plus relaxed margin rules let inexperienced investors (67% without high-school education) buy stocks with borrowed money, inflating a classic bubble; from YouTube analysis videos

event: Ethereum mainnet (Frontier) launches

On July 8, 2015 the Shanghai Composite drops over 5% in a day; Beijing bans sales by executives and >5% shareholders, suspends trading in stocks totaling ~40% of the market, halts IPOs and orders state funds to buy
note: Margin calls forced leveraged investors to sell into the fall, while heavy-handed intervention distorted price signals and eroded confidence as ~$3T of value evaporated; from YouTube analysis videos

event: China yuan devaluation sparks global selloff; Dow opens -1,000 on Aug 24
Google restructures into Alphabet holding company
EU Court of Justice rules Bitcoin exchange VAT-exempt
Gold bottoms near $1,050 as Fed makes first rate hike since 2006
The DAO hacked for ~$60M in ETH; leads to hard fork and ETC split
Brexit vote shock: sharp global selloff, then fast recovery
note: Nikkei -7.9% and DAX -6.8% on the day

event: After the June 23, 2016 Brexit vote the pound plunges about 2% to a 30-year low against the dollar; swaps markets price a 60% chance of a Bank of England rate cut by year-end
note: With the UK running large current-account and fiscal deficits, the currency was the adjustment mechanism that had to fall until investment returned; banks cut 2017 UK growth forecasts and QE re-entry was discussed; from YouTube analysis videos

event: On June 24, 2016 the Dow closes down 611 points, its 8th-largest point loss ever, after the surprise Leave result; Citigroup falls more than 9% as financials lead the rout
note: Polls had predicted Remain, so the vote hit as a shock; money rotated into safe havens with gold and US Treasuries rallying strongly; from YouTube analysis videos

event: Second Bitcoin halving (block reward 25 to 12.5 BTC)
Bitfinex hacked, ~120,000 BTC stolen
Trump election win sparks reflation rally in banks and industrials
note: gold falls as yields and dollar jump

event: ICO boom raises billions; altcoin market cap explodes
note: ETH used as main funding rail

event: Enterprise Ethereum Alliance launched with founding members JPMorgan, Microsoft and Intel, growing past 50 members within six months.
note: Enterprise endorsement helped cement Ethereum as the number two blockchain after Bitcoin despite the DAO setback a year earlier, from YouTube analysis videos

event: The SEC denied the Winklevoss twins' spot Bitcoin ETF application, part of mass ETF rejections in 2017.
note: Regulator argued the largely unregulated crypto market made a formal investment vehicle premature; spot ETFs were only approved in 2024, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Japan recognizes Bitcoin as legal payment method
ICO mania set speed records: TenX raised $80 million in 7 minutes and Bancor amassed $153 million, mostly via ERC-20 tokens on Ethereum.
note: The ERC-20 token standard (formalized September 11, 2017) made launching tokens trivial, channeling billions of speculative capital through Ethereum, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Binance exchange opened to customers worldwide on July 14, 2017.
note: Launched mid ICO boom, it grew within a few years into the largest crypto exchange, expanding global retail access, from YouTube analysis videos

event: On August 1, 2017 Bitcoin underwent a contentious hard fork over the block-size war, splitting into BTC (small blocks) and Bitcoin Cash (BCH, big blocks).
note: Miners and exchanges decided which chain kept the BTC ticker; BTC still ended August around $4,500 despite the split, from YouTube analysis videos

event: China bans ICOs and orders domestic crypto exchanges to close
CBOE and CME launch Bitcoin futures
Bitcoin peaks near $19,800 all-time high
Synchronized global growth: record-low volatility melt-up
note: S&P +19%, Nifty +29%, every S&P month positive on total return

event: Grayscale GBTC traded at ~100% premium to spot BTC at the December top, a marker of retail mania
At the Dec 2017 top ~95% of holders were in profit; first big sell triggered a cascade of profit-taking, stop-losses and leveraged liquidations
The CryptoKitties NFT game overwhelmed the Ethereum network, sending gas fees to extreme levels and delaying transactions.
note: First mass NFT craze (ERC-721 standard) exposed Ethereum's throughput limits at the peak of the bull market, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Beverage maker Long Island Iced Tea renamed itself Long Blockchain Corp and its stock immediately jumped about 200%.
note: Classic mania marker: any association with the word blockchain inflated valuations at the cycle top, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Fed tightening (funds rate ~1.2% to 2.4%) plus balance-sheet runoff through 2018 squeezed liquidity and pressured BTC alongside risk assets
Crypto crash begins; total market cap falls from ~$830B peak
Coincheck hacked for ~$530M in NEM tokens
ETH and altcoins peaked ~1 month after BTC December top as capital rotated from BTC into alts, then altcoins collapsed hardest
Ether sold for about $0.31 in the 2014 ICO traded near $1,100, an over 350,000% gain that fueled the ICO gold rush.
note: Extreme early-ICO returns (NXT +2,000,000%, IOTA +580,000%) drove retail investors into new token sales hoping to repeat them, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Volmageddon: VIX spike ends 2-year calm, first correction since 2016
BTC fell ~70% from ATH to the ~$6,000 support within two months; broader crypto assets lost up to ~80% through 2018
EOS closed a nearly year-long ICO on June 1, 2018, raising a record $4.1 billion.
note: Largest token sale of the ICO era, showing capital kept flowing into new chains even as the broader market fell, from YouTube analysis videos

event: US-China trade war: tariffs imposed on $250B of Chinese goods
Apple becomes first US company worth $1 trillion
Bitcoin Cash hash war; BTC capitulates to ~$3,200 by December
Q4 rout: S&P 500 -19.8% peak-to-trough on Fed hikes and trade war
note: worst December since 1931 until Powell pivot

event: US builders average only ~900,000 single-family homes a year in 2009-2019, about half the prior pace, leaving a chronic shortage estimated at up to 6 million units
Binance hacked for 7,000 BTC (~$40M)
note: covered by SAFU fund, limited damage

event: Facebook announces Libra stablecoin project
Mid-year recovery rally tops near $13,800, then fades
Gold breaks 6-year range above $1,400 on Fed rate-cut turn
Xi Jinping endorses blockchain technology; brief China-driven pump
Google claims quantum supremacy with Sycamore processor
note: 53-qubit chip; ignited investor interest in quantum computing

event: Fed pivot with three rate cuts; S&P +29%, Phase One trade deal signed
Pandemic-era 30-year US mortgage rates hit a record-low 2.65%; home sales jump ~20% above pre-pandemic levels and ~14M owners refinance ($2.6T of refis in 2020 alone)
Before the crash BTC was up ~30% YTD (~$9,300), best start since 2012, on coronavirus safe-haven narrative
Feb 27: Dow -1,191 points, then-biggest point drop in history; fastest-ever 10% correction from record highs in six sessions
COVID-19 Black Thursday crash; BTC drops ~50% to ~$3,850
COVID-19 crash: S&P 500 falls ~34% in 23 trading days
note: fastest bear market in history; circuit breakers hit 4 times

event: Unlimited Fed QE and $2T CARES Act ignite V-shaped recovery
Saudi-Russia output deal collapse sends oil -20% on top of COVID panic; circuit breakers halt trading repeatedly, Dow -13% on Mar 16
Treasuries and gold sell off alongside stocks as leveraged investors dump liquid assets to meet collateral calls; Fed buys $107B of bonds in a single day
WTI oil futures trade negative for first time in history as storage costs exceed the sale price amid collapsed demand
Third Bitcoin halving (reward 12.5 to 6.25 BTC)
Tesla overtakes Toyota as most valuable automaker
MicroStrategy makes first corporate BTC treasury purchase ($250M)
Gold sets record $2,075/oz on pandemic stimulus and negative real yields
Apple becomes first US company worth $2 trillion
China imposes 'three red lines' leverage caps on property developers; most major developers fail the tests and lose access to new credit, choking the debt-fueled building model
PayPal enables buying and selling of crypto for US users
iPhone 12 launches as first 5G iPhone
note: 5G upgrade supercycle across handset and chip suppliers

event: mRNA COVID vaccines show 90%+ efficacy (Pfizer/BioNTech, Moderna)
note: global equity surge; validated mRNA biotech platform

event: Bitcoin breaks 2017 all-time high, passes $20,000
NASDAQ +44% in 2020 on stay-at-home tech boom
Tesla joins S&P 500 as largest company ever added
note: forced index-fund buying; stock rose ~700% in 2020

event: US home prices rise ~20% in one year across 90% of metro areas as record-low rates meet 2-3 months of supply, remote-work migration and peak millennial first-buyer demand
China's overbuilding leaves enough empty apartments to house ~90M people, with ghost cities like Ordos; a Beijing flat costs 50+ years of median income vs ~15 in London
Dogecoin ran from pennies to about $0.70 in the 2021 meme rally, then collapsed roughly 78% to about $0.15 by autumn lows.
note: Retail meme mania amplified by Elon Musk tweets; single Musk tweets did not always pump the price - one day DOGE stayed down 1% despite his 'toddler hodler' tweet, from YouTube analysis videos

event: The CFTC brought an enforcement action against Tether over misrepresented reserves.
note: Years of reserve opacity and the Bitfinex relationship made USDT the case study that shaped later stablecoin regulation, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Meme-stock mania: GameStop short squeeze, retail trading frenzy
GameStop short interest exceeds 140% of float (~90M shares lent and re-lent) and the squeeze drives the stock from ~$19 at the start of January to a peak above $480
note: Chained share lending let shorts exceed shares outstanding; forced buybacks at any price fed a self-reinforcing squeeze, lifting market cap from under $200M in April 2020 to over $28B, from YouTube analysis videos

event: By Jan 29, 2021 GameStop short sellers' losses reach ~$19.75B and Melvin Capital needs a $2.7B bailout led by Citadel
note: Retail-coordinated buying versus concentrated short positions transferred billions from hedge funds; on Jan 26 GME was the most traded US stock with volume matching the five biggest tech names combined, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Jan 28, 2021: Robinhood restricts buying of GameStop (sell-only) after the NSCC clearinghouse demands ~$3B of collateral overnight, and raises over $1B (later $2.4B more) to keep operating
note: Clearinghouse margin requirements on volatile stocks forced brokers to curb buy orders, cutting demand and triggering accusations of one-sided market manipulation, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Tesla discloses $1.5B Bitcoin purchase and plans to accept BTC
Visa settles transactions in USDC stablecoin on Ethereum
note: first major payments network settling in a stablecoin

event: Greensill Capital collapses after credit insurance on its supply-chain loans is not renewed, forcing Credit Suisse to freeze $10B of client funds it had marketed as nearly risk-free
note: Opaque securitized invoice-finance concentrated in a few shaky borrowers unraveled once insurance lapsed, starting the client exodus from Credit Suisse, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Archegos, Bill Hwang's family office, leverages ~$20B of equity roughly 5x into $100B+ of concentrated positions using total return swaps spread across six banks, each unaware of the others
note: Swaps kept his name off filings even while controlling over 50% of ViacomCBS float, and family offices were exempt from hedge-fund disclosure rules, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Mar 22, 2021: ViacomCBS announces a $3B stock and convertible sale; the shares fall 9% then 23%, triggering simultaneous margin calls on Archegos from all its banks
note: The issuer selling into its own inflated rally broke the momentum Archegos had been buying to sustain, and Hwang had no cash left for collateral, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Archegos fire sale: Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs break ranks and dump blocks first (Goldman ~$10B in a day); Credit Suisse, last out, loses $5.5B - the largest margin-default loss in its history - and Nomura ~$2B, with ~$30B wiped out in a week
note: In a multi-bank margin default the first sellers escape and the slowest absorb the crash; Hwang lost ~$20B in two days, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Coinbase lists on Nasdaq; BTC hits ~$64,900 local high
China bans crypto mining; Tesla suspends BTC payments over energy concerns
~1.7 million over-leveraged (up to 100x) accounts liquidated in the April-May crash, flushing speculators and resetting leverage
Coordinated China ban reiteration triggered record BTC inflows to exchanges and panic selling; BTC briefly wicked below $30K
El Salvador adopts Bitcoin as legal tender
Evergrande, with over $300B in liabilities, misses payments on wealth products and a dollar-bond coupon; homebuyer protests erupt and China's offshore property bond market collapses
BITO, first US Bitcoin futures ETF, begins trading
note: fastest ETF to $1B AUM; preceded BTC all-time high

event: Tesla joins $1 trillion club after Hertz orders 100,000 EVs
note: first automaker at $1T; EV mania peak

event: IonQ becomes first pure-play quantum computing public company
note: opened public-market access to the quantum sector

event: Shiba Inu's October 2021 rally threatened to flip Dogecoin's market cap while DOGE ranked 9th at about $35.6 billion and Elon Musk said he holds only BTC, ETH and DOGE.
note: A Finder survey found about 30.6% of US crypto owners held DOGE; meme coins competed for the same retail capital, from YouTube analysis videos

event: BTC hits ~$69,000 and ETH ~$4,870 all-time highs
note: cycle top; futures ETF BITO launched in October

event: Rivian IPO values EV startup above Ford and GM
note: biggest US IPO since Alibaba 2014; EV speculation emblem

event: November ATH was spot-driven with negative perpetual funding rates and ~$750B buy-side volume, ~7x the leverage-heavy April 2021 top
Institutional adoption broadened: Morgan Stanley advisors offered BTC and US pensions bought directly (Fairfax County, Houston firefighters)
Nov 25, 2021: Freddie Reynolds published a Soros-style 'Black Wednesday' blueprint to break UST with ~$1B; Do Kwon dismissed it and taunted billionaires to attack, adding no defenses.
Everything rally: S&P +27% with 70 record closes as inflation hits 7%
Evergrande is formally declared in default for the first time; Beijing signals no bailout, more than a dozen Chinese developers follow, and 1.5M pre-sold homes sit unbuilt
Fed aggressive rate hikes amid high inflation pressure risk assets all year
Tesla sold about 75% of its Bitcoin position, roughly a year after its $1.5 billion purchase.
note: Corporate treasury demand proved reversible; the sale hit sentiment during the 2022 bear market, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Apple becomes first company to touch $3 trillion market cap
note: milestone at the peak before the 2022 rate-hike selloff

event: UST slipped below $1 after the Abracadabra 'Degen Box' leverage scare (aUST-MIM loops); the fright pushed newly formed Luna Foundation Guard to plan a BTC reserve for the peg.
Russia invades Ukraine; energy and commodity shock hits Europe
note: DAX enters bear market territory intraday

event: Meta suffers record ~$232B one-day market cap loss
note: weak guidance and costly metaverse pivot

event: LFG raised $1B from Jump Crypto and Three Arrows via OTC LUNA sales to buy BTC backing 20% of UST supply, finishing ~$3B of purchases with a $1.5B buy on May 5, 2022.
Feb 2022: Fed's Bullard calls for 100bp of hikes by Jul 1; market odds of a 50bp March hike jump to 75% and the 10-year yield tops 2% for the first time since 2019
Gold spikes near $2,070 on war, then fades on surging dollar; flat year
note: held up despite fastest Fed hikes in decades

event: Anchor protocol held ~80% of all UST (~$14B) by paying a fixed ~20% APY subsidized ~$7M/day from a draining yield reserve, the main artificial demand driver for UST.
At the early-April 2022 peak LUNA hit ~$119-120, Terra passed Solana as 6th-largest crypto, and UST (~$18.7B) became the 3rd-largest stablecoin, listed on all major exchanges.
Terra/LUNA and UST stablecoin collapse (~$40B wiped out)
May 7, 2022: right after Terra withdrew UST liquidity from Curve for the 4pool migration, a whale swapped ~$85M UST for USDC (plus reported ~$100-200M sold on Binance), knocking UST off $1.
About $9B of the $14B UST in Anchor was withdrawn within 48 hours of the depeg (Celsius pulled out immediately), and the exit selling drove UST far below its peg.
Death spiral mechanics: honoring the $1 UST-for-LUNA swap forced hyperinflationary minting, LUNA supply grew over 20,000x in days and its price fell from ~$119 weeks earlier to under a cent.
LFG emptied its reserve, selling over 80,000 BTC through market makers on multiple exchanges to defend UST's peg; it failed and the dumping added sell pressure on Bitcoin itself.

Terra validators halted the blockchain twice (cheap LUNA made governance/validator attacks feasible), disabled mint-and-burn, then forked to Terra 2.0 (May 27 snapshot); old chain became Terra Classic.
Celsius freezes withdrawals; Three Arrows Capital collapses
Jul 2022: global tightening broadens - ECB hikes 50bp, Bank of Canada 100bp - while a 2s10s yield-curve inversion of 20bp+ flags recession risk
Aug 10, 2022: Ethereum's Goerli testnet completed the final successful dress-rehearsal merge, firming the mainnet Merge target around Sept 15, 2022.
Ethereum Merge: transition from proof-of-work to proof-of-stake
The Merge cut new ETH issuance ~90% (ending ~13,000 ETH/day mining rewards) and energy use ~99.95% (from ~78 TWh/yr, comparable to Chile), opening a deflationary path via the fee burn.

Staked ETH and rewards stayed locked until the Shanghai upgrade (6-12 months), muting merge-day sell pressure, while miners planned the ETHW proof-of-work fork and traders borrowed ETH for fork airdrops.
Sep 2022: Germany nationalizes Uniper, its biggest importer of Russian gas, with an €8B injection as the utility loses ~€100M per day after Russia halts deliveries
note: Utilities forced to replace contracted Russian gas at spot prices bled cash until the state took them over, a landmark of the 2022 energy crisis, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Sep 2022: Germany unveils a €65B inflation-relief package (€95B including earlier rounds), plans a windfall tax on energy suppliers, and fills gas storage to ~85-90% via record LNG imports with its first LNG terminal due online in December
note: Fiscal support and crash-built LNG infrastructure cushioned industry from the Russian gas cutoff, though stockpiling itself pushed European gas prices higher, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Oct 2022: viral social-media rumors of collapse trigger ~CHF100B of client outflows from Credit Suisse; Q4 withdrawals reach CHF110.5B and managed wealth falls from ~$900B to ~$580B during 2022, with Saudi National Bank injecting $1.5B
note: A fee-based wealth-management model dies with client trust: smartphone-era withdrawals turned rumor into a slow-motion bank run, from YouTube analysis videos

event: FTX exchange collapses and files for bankruptcy
OpenAI launches ChatGPT; ignites AI investment wave
note: start of AI rally driving NASDAQ/S&P in 2023-2025

event: Nov 2: CoinDesk published leaked Alameda balance sheet (~$14B assets mostly in FTX own FTT token), exposing the illiquidity that started the run
Nov 6: Binance announced liquidation of ~$2.1B FTT holdings; FTT fell over 80% in 72 hours; ~$6B withdrawals hit FTX before it froze them
FTX hole revealed: ~$8B liabilities, ~$4B of customer funds misappropriated to prop up Alameda Research
Nov 8-9: Binance signed non-binding letter of intent to acquire FTX then backed out after due diligence, sealing the collapse
ChatGPT launch becomes the catalyst for the AI trade; Nvidia later quantifies it with $11B next-quarter sales guidance
Fed hikes 425bp in 2022, fastest since 1980s: S&P -19%, NASDAQ -33%
note: worst year since 2008; bonds fell simultaneously

event: Central banks buy record ~1,100 tons of gold in 2022 — most since early 1970s — seeking a reserve asset that is no one's liability after sanctions on Russia
Microsoft announces multiyear ~$10B investment in OpenAI
note: anchored the AI arms race among hyperscalers

event: Google Bard demo error wipes ~$100B off Alphabet in a day
note: markets pricing AI competitive position in real time

event: US banking crisis (Silvergate, SVB, Signature); USDC briefly depegs, BTC then rallies
SVB and Credit Suisse failures spark banking scare, quickly contained
SVB's pandemic deposits tripled to $189B and were parked in ~$100B of long-term bonds; Fed rate hikes left a $17B unrealized loss - the bond-duration trap
Mar 8-10, 2023: SVB reveals a $1.8B bond-sale loss; depositors try to pull $42B in one day and regulators seize the bank, later guaranteeing even uninsured deposits
Circle disclosed about 8% of USDC reserves were stuck at collapsed Silicon Valley Bank, knocking USDC off its dollar peg.
note: The federal backstop of SVB's uninsured depositors made Circle the bailout's largest beneficiary and restored the peg - showing bank risk feeds directly into stablecoin risk, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Mar 14-16, 2023: Credit Suisse discloses 'material weaknesses' in its 2021-22 financial reporting, then its largest shareholder Saudi National Bank rules out further support; shares drop ~25% in a day and CS borrows CHF50B from the Swiss National Bank
note: Coming days after SVB's failure, the twin confidence shocks accelerated ~$10B of daily outflows that no liquidity line could offset, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Mar 19, 2023: in a government-brokered weekend rescue UBS buys 167-year-old Credit Suisse for ~CHF3B - about 1% of its 2007 peak value - with over CHF100B of state liquidity and a CHF9B guarantee
note: Regulators forced the merger before Monday's open to prevent a default by a globally systemic bank from spreading the March 2023 banking panic, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Swiss regulator FINMA writes CHF16B (~$17B) of Credit Suisse AT1 bonds down to zero while shareholders still receive ~$3B, inverting the normal creditor hierarchy
note: A Swiss-specific 'viability event' clause let AT1s be wiped on state support regardless of equity, shocking the global bank-bond market and drawing lawsuits, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Nvidia blowout AI guidance ignites AI rally; NASDAQ +43% in 2023
note: Magnificent 7 drive most of S&P gains

event: Nvidia blowout data-center guidance; stock +24% in a day, joins $1T club
note: first hard proof of AI chip demand

event: Nvidia becomes first chip stock to reach $1T market cap; Magnificent Seven coined as gains concentrate — S&P roughly flat excluding seven stocks
SEC sues Binance and Coinbase over securities violations
BlackRock files for spot Bitcoin ETF
Goldman Sachs economists forecast AI adds ~1.5% annual productivity growth for a decade; Nvidia invokes AI ~70 times on its earnings call as retail flow concentrates in Nvidia/AMD/Microsoft/Alphabet
Hyperscaler capex arms race: Microsoft, Google and Meta all guide AI infrastructure spending higher, funneling money to Nvidia while Intel loses server share
Grayscale wins court case against SEC over spot ETF conversion
Oct 7 Hamas attack on Israel starts the gold rally: +$73 in 15 sessions; China retail demand hits record 883 tons in 2023
US 30-year mortgage rates near 8% in late 2023; with over half of owners locked in at or below 4%, sellers stay put and inventory freezes — the 'lock-in effect'
Binance pays $4.3B US settlement; CZ steps down as CEO
Sam Altman fired then reinstated as OpenAI CEO within days
note: governance shock; Microsoft hit record after resolution

event: Gold ends year at record ~$2,078 on rate-cut hopes and central bank buying
note: central banks buy 1,000+ tonnes for second year

event: US home sales fall to a 30-year low near 4 million in 2024 as ~6.5% mortgage rates and record prices sideline buyers; forecasters expect only a slow 2025-26 recovery
US SEC approves spot Bitcoin ETFs (incl. BlackRock IBIT)
Microsoft passes $3 trillion market cap on AI momentum
note: briefly overtook Apple as most valuable company

event: Spot Bitcoin ETFs first trading day (Jan 11) saw over $4.3B in volume with BlackRock IBIT attracting over $1B
Hong Kong High Court orders China Evergrande liquidated on Jan 29, 2024 after failed restructuring of ~$340B in debts; shares tumble and cross-border asset battles begin
Nikkei surpasses 1989 record high of 38,915 after 34 years
note: corporate governance reforms, weak yen, Buffett buying

event: Meta posts record ~$196B single-day market cap gain, first dividend
note: largest one-day value gain in history at the time

event: Nvidia crosses $2 trillion market cap
note: $1T to $2T in about 8 months on AI datacenter demand

event: Bitcoin sets new all-time high ~$73,800 before the halving
BTC available to institutions via OTC desks hit all-time lows amid spot-ETF demand before the April 2024 halving, forcing large buyers onto public exchanges where purchases moved the price directly.
Fourth Bitcoin halving (reward 6.25 to 3.125 BTC)
SEC approves spot Ethereum ETFs (trading began in July)
Nvidia hits $3 trillion, briefly the most valuable company; 10-for-1 split

Jun 4, 2024: BJP's weaker-than-expected election result wipes ~$400B off Indian stocks in a >5% single-day drop, yet the loss is recovered in under three sessions - the fastest rebound in over a decade - and Nifty then sets records in 21 of 22 sessions
note: Exit polls had priced a landslide, so the shortfall shocked; domestic inflows bought the dip once coalition continuity was assured, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Yen carry-trade unwind triggers global selloff; BTC drops to ~$49,000 on Aug 5
Yen carry-trade unwind: Nikkei -12.4% on Aug 5, worst day since 1987
note: triggered by BOJ hike; rebounded 10% next day

event: Aug 2024: a 15bp BOJ hike plus weak US jobs data spike the yen; carry-trade margin calls force selling of US assets - VIX hits 65, S&P -3%, Nasdaq -10% in two weeks
Aug 6, 2024: Nikkei posts one of its biggest one-day gains ever after a BOJ official pledges not to raise rates amid the market turmoil
China stimulus blitz: Shanghai surges over 20% in days
Nifty hits record above 26,000 on domestic inflows; India market cap tops $5T
note: ninth straight positive year before late-2024 pullback

event: Nvidia loses $279B in one day, then-largest single-day cap loss
AI power land-grab: Microsoft signs PPA to reopen Three Mile Island nuclear plant; OpenAI/Oracle install 1GW+ gas turbines at Stargate Abilene; Meta plans 1GW Prometheus and 5GW Hyperion data centers
Gold sets record ~$2,790 on record central bank buying and Fed cuts
Trump wins US election on pro-crypto platform; BTC surges past $90,000
Trump wins election; S&P +23% for year, second straight 20%+ year
Bitcoin crosses $100,000 for the first time
Google unveils Willow quantum chip with breakthrough error correction
note: quantum stocks (IonQ, Rigetti, D-Wave) soared into year-end

event: MicroStrategy joins the Nasdaq-100 index
note: leveraged-Bitcoin proxy entered mainstream index funds

event: Willow (105 qubits, below-threshold error correction) gives Alphabet its best day since April and triggers a speculative rally in small pure-play quantum stocks
DeepSeek releases open-source V3 on Christmas Day, then R1 reasoning model rated on par with OpenAI o1, claiming ~$6M training cost (~3% of OpenAI's spend)
US affordability crisis: a record 242 cities have $1M+ starter homes (up from 80 in 2020), ~65% of households cannot afford a median-priced new home, 35% of sellers cut asking prices
Gold's two-year rally tops 100% to over $4,000/oz, roughly double S&P 500 and Nasdaq gains, as investors hedge debt monetization and fiat-currency debasement
Bubble warning trades: Peter Thiel dumps his entire Nvidia stake and 76% of his Tesla shares, while Michael Burry's portfolio turns to short positions in Nvidia and Palantir
Amazon, Alphabet, Meta and Microsoft pour ~$400B into AI in 2025, mostly data centers; global AI spending is projected to hit $500B in 2026 and $3T/year on power and resources by 2030

Gary Marcus warns an AI correction could resemble 2008 rather than dot-com: overleveraged banks and investors, wildly overvalued assets, and a government too close to the AI club — 'it could all blow up in 2026'
Bubble-scale funding: AI drives ~70% of venture capital activity with ~70,000 AI startups worldwide (vs ~1,300 nine years earlier), including billion-dollar checks for pre-product ideas
MicroStrategy renamed itself Strategy while running the treasury flywheel: selling stock at roughly 2x the value of its bitcoins and issuing zero-coupon convertible bonds to buy more BTC.
note: Stock volatility above Bitcoin's let it issue converts at 0% interest; the premium-to-NAV loop was dubbed an infinite money glitch, from YouTube analysis videos

event: The Bitcoin treasury-company wave went global: over 130 listed firms (up ~170% in a year) held more than 800,000 BTC, over 3% of total supply.
note: UK web firm Smarter Web went from 4 million to over 1 billion pounds in 2 months and Japan's Metaplanet rose ~600% after announcing BTC purchases, from YouTube analysis videos

event: Tether's US Treasury holdings grew larger than those of Germany as stablecoin issuers became major buyers of US debt.
note: Stablecoin reserves (1:1 in cash and short-term Treasuries under the GENIUS Act) turned crypto dollar demand into structural demand for US government debt, from YouTube analysis videos

event: BTC hits ~$109,000 around Trump inauguration; TRUMP memecoin launch
DeepSeek shock: Nvidia -17%, ~$600B market cap wiped in one day
note: largest single-day company value loss in history

event: Jensen Huang says useful quantum computers 15-30 years away; quantum stocks crash
Stargate project announced: up to $500B for US AI datacenters
DeepSeek's cheap AI assistant sparks selloff: Nvidia plunges 17%, shedding ~$600B in market value, the largest one-day cap loss in its history
DeepSeek rout hits chips broadly: semiconductor index has worst day since March 2020 (Broadcom -17%, Micron -13%, ARM -11%), Nasdaq -3% while Dow rises as investors rotate to value
Scale AI CEO Alexandr Wang claims DeepSeek secretly holds ~50,000 Nvidia H100 GPUs despite US export controls, undercutting the pure cheap-training narrative
Wedbush's Dan Ives calls the DeepSeek panic a top-3 tech buying opportunity of the decade: ~$2T enterprise AI capex over 3 years unchanged, and cheaper inference is bullish for software names
Bybit hacked for ~$1.5B, largest crypto theft ever
Hyperscalers guide combined 2025 AI capex above $300B
Microsoft announces Majorana 1 topological-qubit chip and Amazon unveils its first quantum chip; markets barely react — seen as roadmaps not proof
US executive order creates Strategic Bitcoin Reserve
CoreWeave IPO — first major AI-infrastructure listing
note: priced below range amid AI-capex doubts, later rallied

event: Liberation Day tariffs: ~$6.6T lost in two days, S&P -10%, NASDAQ -11%
note: largest two-day dollar loss in history

event: 90-day tariff pause: S&P +9.5% best day since 2008, NASDAQ +12.2%
note: S&P recovered to positive for year by May 13

event: Retaliation wave: China 34% tariff on all US imports, EU plans up to 50%, Canada 25% on $42B; JPMorgan lifts global recession odds to 60%
Circle (USDC issuer) IPO surges ~168% on debut
note: stablecoin business validated by public markets

event: 2025 US construction spending falls in most sectors but rises for data centers and power stations; utilities and picks-and-shovels construction stocks outperform
GENIUS Act signed into law: first US federal stablecoin framework
Nvidia becomes first company worth $4 trillion
note: Microsoft followed to $4T at end of July

event: Powell's Jackson Hole signal of rate cuts despite above-target inflation sends gold vertical toward $4,000; dollar posts weakest first half in 50 years
Study of $30-40B in enterprise generative-AI investment finds 95% of organizations report zero measurable ROI; large-company AI adoption appears to peak in 2025
GPT-5 launch reignites AI investment rush with S&P 500 at record highs, while gold simultaneously climbs to all-time highs — betting on AI and hedging its crash at once
Anthropic raises at $183B valuation
note: AI lab valuations tripling within a year

event: Alphabet crosses $3 trillion market cap
note: favorable antitrust ruling and Gemini AI momentum

event: Nvidia announces plan to invest up to $100B in OpenAI
Harvard economist Jason Furman estimates AI-related investment accounted for ~92% of US GDP growth in H1 2025 — ex-AI the economy grew just 0.1%
Bitcoin sets all-time high ~$126,000
Oct 10 tariff shock: 100% China tariff announcement triggers record ~$19B liquidations
Gold breaks $4,000/oz for first time on tariff fears and central bank demand
AMD-OpenAI multi-gigawatt GPU deal; AMD jumps ~24%
note: validated a second supplier in the AI chip race

event: Nvidia becomes first company to close above $5 trillion
note: $1T to $5T in under 2.5 years on AI demand

event: China rare-earth export controls prompt threat of massive tariff hike; ends 33-session streak without a 1% S&P move
Circular-deal anxiety peaks: Nvidia-OpenAI-Oracle-CoreWeave-AMD vendor-financing web; Morgan Stanley pegs 2025-2028 data-center spend at $3T with ~$800B from private credit
Meta raises $30B in corporate bonds plus ~$30B off-balance-sheet via a structured JV to fund AI data centers — AI buildout shifts from equity-funded to debt-financed
OpenAI hits ~$20B revenue run rate but ~$17B net loss in 2025; expects annual losses through 2028 with breakeven around 2029-2030
Concentration risk flagged: ~52% of S&P 500 market cap in 20 mostly AI-driven companies; AI names account for ~75% of index returns
S&P recovers to record 6,932; +17.9% total return, third straight double-digit year
note: Nvidia, Alphabet, Microsoft, Broadcom lead; AI capex surge

event: DAX rallies to records on German defense and infrastructure spending boom
BTC ended 2025 near $87,000, down over 30% from October ATH; Q4 2025 was worst fourth quarter since 2018 (about -24%)
Fed officially ended quantitative tightening on Dec 1, 2025; the year tight liquidity and high real yields had capped the rally
Google Quantum AI publicly shortens its timeline to ~5 years for a first practical quantum application, contradicting the 15-30 year view and re-exciting quantum investors
US market splits: median sale price up only ~2% YoY while prices fall in 77 of the 300 largest metros, led by Florida, Texas and California; foreclosure filings hit a six-year high
New Zealand's post-2022 bust shows the downside: real house prices ~31% below peak, back at 2016 levels, and over 40% of Auckland apartment sellers take a loss in Q1 2026

Big Tech sheds hundreds of billions in early 2026 (Amazon -$343B, Apple -$256B, Alphabet -$88B) as investors doubt near-term AI profits versus soaring capex, and the touted $100B Nvidia-OpenAI deal quietly stalls
'Data wall' risk flagged in the 2026 bubble debate: AI models are predicted to exhaust all human-generated training data by ~2027, leaving progress dependent on unproven synthetic data
Gold hits all-time high $5,589/oz on Jan 28
note: central banks buy 244t net in Q1 2026, above 5-yr average

event: AI data centers trigger a memory-chip crunch: DRAM prices jump 80-90% between Dec 2025 and Jan 2026; Musk and Cook warn of shortages, Sony may delay the next PlayStation to 2028-29
Post-peak correction deepens; BTC trades near $67,500, ~47% below ATH
Strategy bought 17,994 BTC for $1.28 billion (about $70,946 each) in a single week of the bear market, lifting holdings to 738,731 BTC, about 3.4% of supply, at $75,862 average cost.
note: Preferred-equity ATM issuance (the 'Stretch' instrument) let it raise ~$256 million per day even with Bitcoin ~45% below its all-time high, from YouTube analysis videos

event: The 20 millionth bitcoin was mined, leaving only about 1 million BTC to be created over the next ~14 years.
note: Over 95% of the 21 million hard cap now circulates, shifting miner income toward fees and underlining the fixed-supply thesis, from YouTube analysis videos

event: CLARITY Act passes Senate Banking Committee, briefly lifting sentiment
US spot Bitcoin ETFs post worst month on record with ~$4.5B outflows
New Fed chair Warsh holds rates and removes expected cut; BTC hits 21-month low near $58,000
AI overinvestment fears: sharp selloff, Kospi -10%, NASDAQ -4.6% on week
note: chip and AI-infrastructure names lead declines

event: Nikkei hits record ~72,300, third straight month of record highs, +35% H1
Despite June swoon, Nasdaq 100 +20% in H1 2026 on AI infrastructure spend
Tech leads H1 2026 gains; semiconductor suppliers outperform US megacaps
note: AI trade broadened internationally; volatility at end of June

event: In the correction toward $60K whale wallets hit a 2026 high and long-term holders turned net buyers while retail panic-sold; BTC underperformed gold
Jun 2026: BOJ hikes to 1%, highest since 1995, yet the yen hits 162/USD (weakest since 1986) despite a record 11.7 trillion yen ($73B) intervention funded by US Treasury sales
